titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Lee Friedlander Papers
titlestmt.author: by Michael Rush and Leigh Golden

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 9728416.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday December 29, 2017 at 20:20

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The papers of Lee Friedlander include photographs, correspondence, work files, book projects, printed materials, awards, objects, audiovisual materials, and computer disks documenting his career as a photographer, including his work process, exhibitions, publications, business activities, and honors. Photographs include color slide portraits of musicians; black and white slides; exhibition prints of western landscapes; and work prints corresponding to exhibition prints held by the Beinecke Library, Yale University, and the Yale University Art Gallery. The collection also includes personal papers documenting his relationships with family and friends, in particular his wife Maria Friedlander.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition note
acqinfo.p: Purchased from the Lee and Maria Friedlander Family Foundation on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund and the Frederick W. and Carrie S. Beinecke Fund for Western Americana, 2010.

accessrestrict.head: Information about Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 1-8a (color slides): Restricted until twenty years after Lee Friedlander's death. A detailed list of color slides is in the curator's files.
Boxes 127-129 (audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 130 (computer disks): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

userestrict.head: Ownership Copyright
userestrict.p: The Lee Friedlander Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.
Boxes 14-49 (work prints): May not be exhibited or reproduced.

prefercite.head: Cite As
prefercite.p: Lee Friedlander Papers. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Notes
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections [as they are acquired], and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

bioghist.head: Lee Friedlander (1934-)
bioghist.p: Lee Friedlander, born July 14, 1934, is an American photographer. Friedlander studied photography with Edward Kaminski at the Art Center School, Los Angeles, California. Friedlander has been a freelance photographer since 1955. In 1990, the MacArthur Foundation awarded Friedlander with MacArthur Fellowship. A major retrospective of his work was held at the Museum of Modern Art, New York, 2005. He is married to Maria Friedlander.

scopecontent.head: Description of the Papers
scopecontent.p: The papers of Lee Friedlander include photographs, correspondence, work files, book projects, printed materials, awards, objects, audiovisual materials, and computer disks documenting his career as a photographer, including his work process, exhibitions, publications, business activities, and honors. Photographs include color slide portraits of musicians; black and white slides; exhibition prints of western landscapes; and work prints corresponding to exhibition prints held by the Beinecke Library, Yale University, and the Yale University Art Gallery. The collection also includes personal papers documenting his relationships with family and friends, in particular his wife Maria Friedlander.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into six series: I. Photographs, 1950-2009. II. Correspondence, 1958-1990. III. Work Files, 1960-2010. IV. Book Projects, 1976-2006. V. Printed Materials, 1960-2010. VI. Awards and Objects, 1980-2009. VII. Audiovisual Materials, 1983-2007.

arrangement.head: Arrangement note
arrangement.p: Photographs are arranged into four subseries: Color slides, Black and white slides, Exhibition prints, and Work prints. Exhibition and work prints are listed in the order in which they were kept and listed by Lee Friedlander.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Color slides include 35mm and 120mm slides and primarily consist of portraits of jazz, country and western, rock, folk and soul musicians active in the late 1950s and early 1960s. They also include potraits of wrestlers and photographs of Iroquois crafts.

Black and white slides were received in a box labelled "Slides returned from Arles" and consist of approximatley 850 slides of photographs by Friedlander. Slides are stored in binders and a slide boxes. Some of the pages of slides in the binders are labelled, including "New Portrait," "Nude," "Maria," "Cherry Blossom," "Desert," "Self Portrait," "New Self Portrait," and "Urban."
Exhibition prints include photographs from "Tetons, 1999" and western landscapes. Exhibition prints are 16" x 20".

Work prints include photographs from many of Lee Friedlander's publications, as well as other categories as assembled by Friedlander. The work prints are correspond with exhibitio prints held by the Beinecke Library of the Yale University Art Gallery. Works and series represented include the following: America by Car , Apples and Olives , At Work , The Desert Seen , Dreyfus, Fashion, Olmstead Landscapes, Pickup Beds, Stems , and "Tetons, 1999," among others. Work prints are 11" x 14".

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access note
accessrestrict.p: Access to color slides is restricted for twenty years after the death of Lee Friedlander.
Work prints may not be exhibited or reproduced.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access note
accessrestrict.p: Access to color slides is restricted for twenty years after the death of Lee Friedlander.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Removed from a box labelled "Slides retruned from Arles".

arrangement.head: Arrangement note
arrangement.p: Exhibition prints are organized into two groups: "Tetons, 1999" and Western Landscapes. Each group is listed in their original order as received from Friedlander, which is chronological.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Titles are transcribed from the reverse of each print, with the print number in parentheses.
Dates noted separate from print titles record the year the print was created.

arrangement.head: Arrangement note
arrangement.p: Work prints are arranged alphabetically by title/category as assigned by Lee Friedlander. Within each group prints are listed in their original order as received from Friedlander.

processinfo.head: General note
processinfo.p: Titles are transcribed from the reverse of each print, with the print number in parentheses. The negative number is noted when present.
Dates are derived from negative numbers and represent the year the photograph was taken.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access note
userestrict.p: Work prints may not be exhibited or reproduced.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: The "original 78" refers to a group of exhibition prints that Lee Friedlander loaned to the Yale Art Gallery prior to the purchase of his collection. Because the prints cut across his works and topical categories ( Sticks and Stones ; The Desert Seen ; Tetons, etc.), Lee Friedlander used the functional description "original 78" to refer to this group.

did.unittitle: Portraits and self-portraits from Sticks and Stones
Portraits and self-portraits from America by Car
Carol Rovane, Akeel Bilgrami, Zarina Bilgrami (1784-25)

arrangement.head: Arrangement note
arrangement.p: Correspondence is arranged in two subseries: personal and business.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: The bulk of the personal correspondence consists of letters sent by Lee Friedlander to his wife Maria while travelling. It also includes letters from Maria Friedlander to Lee, as well as letters from their children and other unidentified correspondents.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Business corresponence consists primarily of letters from galleries and presses regarding exhibits and publications. Extensive related material is included in Series III. Work Files.

arrangement.head: Arrangement note
arrangement.p: Beinecke staff arranged the work files into a rough chronological sequence. Files are grouped into folders with approximate year spans. Folders are organized into boxes by decade.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Lee Friedlander's work files consist of a mix of correspondence, clippings, photocopies, printed material, and some photographs by others. Work files document a range of activities and areas of Friedlander's life: exhibitions, travel, publications, friends and family, business, awards and honors, etc.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Archival Materials note
relatedmaterial.p: Additional related materials are in Series I. Photographs, Series II. Correspondence, and Series V. Printed Material.

arrangement.head: Arrangement note
arrangement.p: Book projects are arranged alphabetically by title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Book project files consist of book dummies, drafts, and corrected proofs for books by Lee Friedlander.

arrangement.head: Arrangement note
arrangement.p: Printed materials are arranged by type. Within each type there is little order to the printed materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Printed materials consist of exhibition catalogs and notices, magazines and other periodicals, books, calendars, posters, tear sheets, and album covers. The bulk of the exhibition materials, magazines, periodicals, and books relate in some way to Friedlander's photography. The calendars and posters all feature works by Friedlander. The tear sheets and album covers include images from the color slides in Series I. Photographs.

arrangement.head: Arrangement note
arrangement.p: Awards and Objects is arranged in two subseries.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Awards and objects consist of honorary degrees, medals, awards, and other recogitions received by Lee Friedlander, as well as objects relating to exhibitions of his work or displaying his photographs. Also included is a box of name tags from events attended or worked at by Lee Friedlander.

did.unittitle: American Academy of Arts and Sciences
Art Center College of Design, honorary degree
Friends of Photography Award; La Medaille de la Ville de Paris
Massachusetts College of Art, honorary doctorate
Royal Photographic Society of Great Britain
Banner, Lee Friedlander Totes les mirades, Obra Social, Fundació "la Caixa"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Includes the following medals: Ordre des Arts et des Lettres; Erna Och Victor Hasselblads Medal, 2005; Skowhegan Medal for Photography, 2000.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Includes the following medals: Fluctuat Nec Mergitur, Paris, 1981; Edward MacDowell, 1986; Royal Photographic Society of Great Britain, 2003.

did.unittitle: Statue, International Center of Photography, 2006 Infinity Award
T-shirt, People Working, Cleveland Museum of Art

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Bottle label includes photograph by Lee Friedlander.

arrangement.head: Arrangement note
arrangement.p: Audiovisual materials are organized by media type: VHS cassettes, other video formats, audio cassettes, and other audio formats. Within each format materials are arranged alphabetically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: This series consists of both video and audio recordings, including interviews with Lee Friedlander, awards presentations, published recordings featuring Friedlander's photography on the packaging, and music.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: "American Musicians," with Lee Friedlander, directed by Charles Thompson. Running time: 3:02. Project: BookVideos.

The Arthur Lipsett Project, DVD and papers

Szarkowski keynote speech at SPE Conference March 1988
Texas Bound, Stories by Texas Writers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Box illustration includes photograph by Lee Friedlander.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Box illustration includes photograph by Lee Friedlander.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Computer files consist of discs removed from Series III. Work Files.
